{"key_event":"Third Reform Act and mass party mobilization","reasoning":"The expanded male electorate, mass party organization, Irish tenant/nationalist mobilization, labor protest, women’s property rights, and elected county councils pushed an already dense civil society into a still stronger position, though women’s exclusion and Irish coercion limited a larger jump. State power also rose modestly through compulsory education, tighter election regulation, Irish coercion/public-order powers, and more professional local administration, but it remained strongly checked by Parliament, parties, press, and organized society.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":9.1,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":9.1}
